# UC-14.2.17 · ICS Network Segmentation Violations

## Description

Hosts in wrong VLANs or east-west traffic across zones violates Purdue model and IEC 62443.

## Value

Hosts in wrong VLANs or east-west traffic across zones violates Purdue model and IEC 62443.

## Implementation

Maintain `allowed_pair` lookup for zone-to-zone. Alert on any deny or unexpected allow. Quarterly review.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Firewall, switch NetFlow, ARP tables.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="pan:traffic"` `zone_pair` or `sourcetype="flow:ics"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Maintain `allowed_pair` lookup for zone-to-zone. Alert on any deny or unexpected allow. Quarterly review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype="flow:ics"
| where src_zone!=dest_zone AND allowed_pair="false"
| stats count by src, dest, dest_port, app
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**ICS Network Segmentation Violations** — Hosts in wrong VLANs or east-west traffic across zones violates Purdue model and IEC 62443.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="pan:traffic"` `zone_pair` or `sourcetype="flow:ics"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Firewall, switch NetFlow, ARP tables. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: flow:ics. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype="flow:ics". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where src_zone!=dest_zone AND allowed_pair="false"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src, dest, dest_port, app** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when the process is in a known state. Compare key fields and alarm states to the SCADA or DCS HMI and, where relevant, the engineering tool for the PLC. Confirm sourcetypes and field extractions match the vendor feed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Sankey (zones), Table (violations), Single value (open violations).

## SPL

```spl
index=network sourcetype="flow:ics"
| where src_zone!=dest_zone AND allowed_pair="false"
| stats count by src, dest, dest_port, app
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Sankey (zones), Table (violations), Single value (open violations).
